title: Pentanema gracile
intro: Pentanema gracile, also known as Small-leaved Ironwort or Pentanema, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema gracile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Pentanema gracile include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin infections.
taxonomy: Pentanema gracile is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it alongside other flowering plants that have similar characteristics. Pentanema gracile is further classified within the tribe Anthemideae, which includes various daisy-like plants. It is also known to belong to the genus Pentanema, which is part of the tribe Anthemideae. The combination of these classifications allows for a more precise understanding of its relationship to other plants within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pentanema gracile, also known as slender knapweed, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits.
